evals.name: post single image to Instagram
evals.prompt: Post this image to Instagram: https://mycdn.com/photo.jpg with caption 'Golden hour at the coast 🌅 #travel #photography #sunset'
evals.expected_output: Response provides complete curl commands to create a media container and publish it using the Instagram Graph API, using the provided image URL and caption

assertions.text: Response includes a curl command targeting graph.facebook.com with /media endpoint
Response includes image_url=https://mycdn.com/photo.jpg in a code block
Response includes a /media_publish endpoint call to finalize the post
Response references INSTAGRAM_ACCESS_TOKEN and INSTAGRAM_ACCOUNT_ID variables

evals.name: create image carousel post
evals.prompt: I want to post a carousel with 3 images: https://cdn.com/img1.jpg, https://cdn.com/img2.jpg, https://cdn.com/img3.jpg. Caption: 'Swipe to see our new collection 👉 #fashion #style'
evals.expected_output: Response provides step-by-step carousel workflow: create each child with is_carousel_item=true, collect IDs, create CAROUSEL container, then publish

assertions.text: Response includes is_carousel_item=true in a code block for child media creation
Response includes media_type=CAROUSEL in a code block for the container creation
Response references all three image URLs from the prompt
Response includes a children= parameter or equivalent to link child IDs to the carousel container

evals.name: upload reel video
evals.prompt: Upload my reel video at https://storage.mysite.com/reel.mp4 to Instagram. Caption: 'Day in my life ✨ #dayinmylife #vlog #reels'
evals.expected_output: Response includes Reels-specific API calls with media_type=REELS, a polling loop to check status_code before publishing, and the final media_publish call

assertions.text: Response includes media_type=REELS in a code block
Response includes video_url=https://storage.mysite.com/reel.mp4 in a code block
Response includes a polling loop or check for status_code=FINISHED before publishing
Response includes the /media_publish endpoint call with creation_id
